page: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2151.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.0 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Text processing is a notable highlight, scoring 2975.0 in Geekbench, which ranks in the top 10% tier. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 8641.92 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 79.76 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the hardware processes text generation for a small 135M LLM model at a strong rate of 269.48 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the c8id.2xlarge offers a high-compute, low-latency storage profile. The inclusion of a dedicated 474 GB NVMe SSD provides cost-efficient local scratch space without requiring external block storage attachments, though the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. This hardware configuration is suited for compute-intensive workloads that require fast local storage, such as software compilation, text processing pipelines, single-threaded data compression, and web serving.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, and a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2151.0 and text processing reaching the top 10% tier at 2975.0. While multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average, the instance excels at single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation. The 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads needing fast local storage, such as software compilation, text processing, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers strong single-core performance and high-speed local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance featuring 16 GB of DDR5 RAM and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining high-frequency Intel processors with fast local NVMe storage for intensive single-threaded and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Bundled 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing speeds
Optimized for software compilation, data compression, and web serving
